seo_title: ArXiv to ban researchers with AI 'slop' in papers
meta_description: Academic platform ArXiv introduces new policy to reduce AI-generated content in research papers.
body_html:
ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers with 'incontrovertible evidence of AI slop'.

New Policy to Reduce AI-Generated Content

Authors can appeal ban decisions. Policies apply only to cases of incontrovertible evidence. Internal process requires moderator and Section Chair confirmation before imposing penalties.

Annotated bibliographies with no substantial discussion of open research issues. Peer-reviewed articles and position papers must be published if accepted at a conference or journal.

The advent of large language models has made it easy to churn out content on demand. Most review articles are little more than annotated bibliographies with no substantial discussion of open research issues.

source.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
source.summary: ArXiv, a popular platform for preprint academic research, is taking a new step to attempt to reduce the volume of papers that include AI slop. If a paper has "incontrovertible evidence that the authors did not check the results of LLM generation," such as hallucinated references or "meta-comments" left by an LLM, authors will be […]
